prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I have two runs of the same long-running task on disk. Find them: the un-packaged
prompts_findings.phase: Phase 1 — Diff and name the gaps
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude will narrate a reading plan across both runs (2-4 sentences) then surface a structured diff across the four named dimensions with evidence quoted from artefacts.

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-step orchestration instruction (read repo state, read transcripts, walk four dimensions, rank) guarantees Claude narrates its reading plan before producing any quoted evidence. >70% of runs open with 2-4 planning sentences.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble. Start with the first quoted moment.' before the four-dimension list.

risks_fired.evidence: No explicit 'don't soften' anchor on damage rankings. Claude will hedge assessments of the packaged run's failures and under-rank their severity.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Name the damage to a future run concretely. Don't hedge severity.' to the ranking instruction.

failure_modes: Student reads a 4-sentence narration of what Claude will do before evidence appears, setting a passive-consumption rhythm
Ranked gap list understates damage: 'minor drift' replaces 'verifier missed the core quality bar'
recovery_paths: Add 'No preamble. First output is a quoted moment.' to suppress planning narration
Pin ranking language: 'Rate by damage to a future run. If it's major, say so.'

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Read ./CLAUDE.local.md. Read this session's scrollback: the gap list I just rank
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude reads CLAUDE.local.md and scrollback, identifies the one rule the diagnosis killed, edits the file in place, and shows the removed line with a two-sentence rationale.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Cut it from ./CLAUDE.local.md in place' — no explicit preserve-vs-replace boundary for the rest of the file; Claude may rewrite the entire file rather than performing a surgical line removal.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Remove only that line. Leave every other line unchanged.'

risks_fired.evidence: Modifying a named config file without 'no confirmation' instruction; Claude will pause and ask 'Should I overwrite?' before cutting.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Edit the file directly. No confirmation step.'

failure_modes: Claude rewrites CLAUDE.local.md structurally rather than removing a single line, corrupting other rules
Claude asks for overwrite confirmation, breaking the exercise flow mid-session
recovery_paths: Add 'Remove only that line; every other line stays exactly as written'
Add 'Edit in place. No confirmation needed.'

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Author a session-shaper as a personal skill. The skill's job is to shape future s
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude acknowledges the three shapes, asks which shape the student wants, then begins a one-question-at-a-time interview covering the named dimensions before drafting SKILL.md.

risks_fired.evidence: Body callout at exercise line 83 applies the carve-out: 'If Claude shows you the full question list up front instead of asking one at a time, ask it to use the AskUserQuestion tool, or to ask one and wait. Your call.' Callout is within 2 lines of the prompt close. Downgraded to low per body-callout carve-out.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No action needed — body callout is the legal alternative shape per catalog rule.

risks_fired.evidence: 'When you have enough, propose a skill name. Write ~/.claude/skills/<proposed-name>/SKILL.md… Show me before saving.' — Claude's proposed structure is typically accepted without substantive push-back; 30-70% of students approve the first draft.
risks_fired.fix_hint: After 'Show me before saving' add: 'Name the one thing you'd change if this were a colleague's skill, not ours.' Forces engagement before student can approve.

failure_modes: Student approves Claude's first SKILL.md draft without reading the weakest assumption; skill ships generic
recovery_paths: Add a forced-engagement step before approval: ask Claude to name one assumption likely to be wrong

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Before I ship this skill, critique it. Read the SKILL.md you just wrote. Tell me
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude names 2-3 weaknesses in the just-authored SKILL.md with specific line references, flagging the most generic assumption and one Phase 1 failure moment that didn't make it in.

risks_fired.evidence: Self-critique of just-produced SKILL.md in the same conversation. 'Don't reassure me. Name weak parts.' partially suppresses framing but cannot override RLHF self-charity on critique magnitude. >70% of runs produce under-critique ('worth watching' vs 'assumption is likely wrong'). Residual-by-design per compounded/2026-05-02-pedagogy-debrief-prompts-residual-med-risk-by-design.md — verdict stays REVISE.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Be harsher than necessary. Flag at least two things you'd rewrite if this were a colleague's skill.' Alternatively dispatch critique as fresh sub-task with SKILL.md pasted cold.

risks_fired.evidence: Claude names weak parts at reduced magnitude — categorical softening not fully addressed by 'Don't reassure me'. The opening of the critique response will soften before flagging.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Start with the weakest part. Don't open with what's strong.' to reorder the response.

failure_modes: Critique output names 1 weak part at 'worth watching' severity when 2-3 assumptions are genuinely wrong; student treats the skill as polished
Opening sentence reassures before critiquing, framing the critique as fine-tuning rather than substantive gaps
recovery_paths: Add 'Be harsher than necessary; flag at least two things you'd rewrite if this were someone else's work'
Dispatch as fresh sub-task via Task tool with only SKILL.md content, no authoring context

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Invoke the skill we just authored -- by its name -- on the M5 packaged re-run. No
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude invokes the skill on M5 artefacts, produces skill output (pass/fail + ranked findings), then in the same response self-grades whether the skill caught the dominant gap.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Then, in the same response, answer: is the skill any good?' — skill invocation AND self-grading in a single turn. Claude will report the skill 'catches the core pattern' when an independent reader would find substantive gaps. Body callout at lines 116-117 acknowledges this as deliberate convenience tradeoff; per check_pedagogy.md rule 50 and residual-by-design note, verdict stays REVISE.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Split into two prompts: (a) invoke and produce output; (b) 'Now read that output as if you'd never written the skill. What did it miss?' Body already offers this as opt-in — no change needed if the residual is accepted.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Does it miss things a staff engineer reviewing this run would catch?' — asked immediately after producing output in the same session. Claude names 1 partial miss and concludes 'mostly solid' vs the 2-3 misses a fresh reader would identify.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Dispatch the self-assessment as a sub-task via Task tool with only the skill output and dominant gap pasted cold — no authoring context.

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-part prompt (invoke + produce + self-assess in one turn); Claude will narrate the two-step plan before invoking.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No narration. Invoke and show output immediately.' before the produce instruction.

failure_modes: Skill self-grade concludes 'catches the dominant gap' when an independent reviewer would find 2 substantive misses
Opening narration ('I'll now invoke the skill and then assess...') eats the student's highest-attention slot before the invocation begins
recovery_paths: Run as two prompts — invoke first, then fresh-read the output for gaps
Task-tool dispatch for the assessment step, blinded to authoring context
